title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.imageAlt: Close-up of juicy, smoky Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled on a platter with lemon wedges and cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich flavors of India with our homemade Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled, perfect for a flavorful dinner.

description: Experience the quintessential taste of India with our authentic Tandoori Chicken, marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and aromatic spices. This recipe brings the smoky, succulent flavors of a traditional charcoal grill right to your backyard, resulting in perfectly charred and juicy chicken. A true culinary delight that promises a vibrant burst of flavors in every bite, making it an ideal centerpiece for any Indian dinner.

ingredients.notes: whole chicken cut into 8 pieces or bone-in thighs/drumsticks, skin removed

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Make deep gashes on the chicken pieces with a sharp knife. This helps the marinade penetrate the meat, ensuring maximum flavor.

instructions.title: First Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, salt, and half of the red chili powder. Rub this mixture thoroughly over the chicken pieces. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Second Marinade
instructions.text: In another bowl, whisk together plain yogurt, remaining red chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, turmeric powder, mustard oil, and optional food color. Mix until well combined and smooth.

instructions.title: Combine and Marinate
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces from the first marinade into the second yogurt mixture. Ensure every piece is well coated with the thick marinade. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, preferably overnight, for the best flavor and tender texture.

instructions.title: Prepare the Grill
instructions.text: Set up your charcoal grill for direct medium-high heat. Once the coals are hot and covered with a light ash, clean the grill grates thoroughly and lightly oil them to prevent the chicken from sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the hot grill. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until nicely charred and cooked through. Baste with any remaining marinade or a little oil during the last few minutes of cooking for extra moisture and flavor.

instructions.title: Check Doneness and Serve
instructions.text: The chicken is done when its internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) and the juices run clear. Remove from grill, sprinkle with chaat masala, garnish with fresh cilantro and lemon wedges. Serve hot immediately with naan, rice, or a fresh salad.

tips: For truly authentic Tandoori flavor, ensure your chicken marinates for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight. This allows the spices to deeply penetrate the meat, resulting in maximum tenderness and taste.

Kashmiri red chili powder is key for the vibrant red color without excessive heat. If unavailable, a mix of paprika and a smaller amount of regular red chili powder can be used. Avoid adding too much water to the marinade; it should be thick.

Serve your Tandoori Chicken immediately off the grill for the best experience. It pairs wonderfully with mint chutney, sliced onions, lemon wedges, and warm naan bread or jeera rice. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container for up to 2-3 days and reheated gently.
article:
**Savor the Smoky Perfection: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled for Your Indian Dinner**

There are few dishes that capture the essence of Indian cuisine quite like Tandoori Chicken. Its vibrant red hue, succulent tenderness, and deeply spiced flavor are iconic, but when it’s prepared over hot charcoal, it reaches an unparalleled level of smoky perfection. Far more than just a meal, Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is an experience – a culinary journey to the bustling streets and grand kitchens of India, right in your backyard. If you’re ready to elevate your dinner game and impress your taste buds, read on to discover the secrets behind this beloved Indian classic.

## What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?

At its heart, Tandoori Chicken is a dish originating from the Punjab region of India and Pakistan. Traditionally, it's cooked in a "tandoor," a cylindrical clay oven that uses charcoal or wood fire to reach incredibly high temperatures. The intense, radiant heat of the tandoor cooks the chicken quickly, sealing in juices while imparting a distinctive smoky char. When we talk about Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled, we're essentially replicating this traditional method using a conventional charcoal grill. This allows home cooks to achieve that authentic smoky flavor and crispy, slightly charred exterior that's characteristic of true tandoori cooking, distinguishing it from oven-baked or pan-fried versions. The chicken, usually bone-in pieces, is marinated in a rich mixture of yogurt and a potent blend of spices, which not only tenderizes the meat but also infuses it with layers of aromatic flavor before it hits the fiery coals.

## Ingredients for Tandoori Chicken

Creating the perfect Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled dish starts with selecting the right ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need:

*   **For the Chicken:**
    *   8-10 pieces of chicken (thighs and drumsticks work best, skin removed)
    *   2 lemons (juiced)
    *   Salt to taste
*   **For the Marinade:**
    *   1 cup plain full-fat yogurt (Greek yogurt or hung curd for thicker marinade)
    *   2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste
    *   2 teaspoons red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, or regular for heat)
    *   1 teaspoon turmeric powder
    *   1 tablespoon ground coriander
    *   1 teaspoon ground cumin
    *   1 teaspoon garam masala
    *   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
    *   1 tablespoon fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
    *   1-2 tablespoons mustard oil (or any neutral oil)
    *   Red food coloring (optional, for the traditional vibrant color)
*   **For Grilling & Garnish:**
    *   Oil for basting
    *   Fresh coriander leaves, chopped
    *   Onion rings
    *   Lemon wedges

## How to Make Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled (Step-by-Step)

Follow these steps to create succulent Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled at home:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry. Using a sharp knife, make 2-3 deep slits on each piece of chicken. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply and ensures even cooking.
2.  **First Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with the juice of one lemon and salt. Rub it all over the chicken, making sure to get into the slits. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes. This step helps tenderize the chicken and draws out excess moisture.
3.  **Prepare the Second Marinade:** In another bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, ground coriander, ground cumin, garam masala, black pepper, crushed fenugreek leaves, mustard oil, and optional red food coloring. Mix everything thoroughly until you have a smooth, thick paste.
4.  **Second Marinade (The Magic Happens):** Drain any liquid from the first marinated chicken. Add the chicken pieces to the yogurt-spice mixture. Using your hands, generously coat each piece, ensuring the marinade gets into all the slits. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or ideally, overnight (up to 24 hours) for the best flavor and tenderness.
5.  **Prepare Your Charcoal Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before you plan to cook, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal for two-zone cooking (direct and indirect heat). You want a medium-high heat (coals glowing red with a light layer of ash). Clean and oil your grill grates.
6.  **Grilling the Chicken:** Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the medium-high heat. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side to get a nice char. Then, move the chicken to the indirect heat zone, close the lid, and cook for another 20-30 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) at the thickest part. Flip and baste the chicken with a little oil every 5-7 minutes to keep it moist and prevent burning.
7.  **Finishing Touches:** For the last 5 minutes, you can move the chicken back over direct heat if you want more charring, watching it closely to prevent burning.
8.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. Squeeze fresh lemon juice over it, garnish with chopped coriander, and serve hot.

## Tips for Perfect Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

Achieving that perfect Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is an art, but these tips will help you master it:

*   **Quality Chicken:** Use fresh, good quality bone-in chicken pieces (thighs and drumsticks are excellent choices for flavor and moisture). Removing the skin helps the marinade penetrate better and prevents excessive flare-ups on the grill.
*   **Thick Yogurt:** Use full-fat plain yogurt or, even better, hung curd (yogurt drained of excess whey). A thicker marinade adheres better to the chicken and prevents it from becoming watery.
*   **Marination Time is Key:** Do not skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is truly best.
*   **Score Deeply:** Ensure your slits on the chicken are deep enough to allow the marinade to infuse the meat thoroughly.
*   **Control Grill Temperature:** Start with medium-high direct heat for searing, then finish on indirect heat. This creates a beautifully charred exterior without drying out the interior. Don't overcrowd the grill, as this lowers the temperature and steams the chicken instead of grilling it.
*   **Basting:** Basting with a little oil or leftover marinade (if not contaminated) helps keep the chicken moist and promotes browning.
*   **Rest the Chicken:** Just like any grilled meat, letting the Tandoori Chicken rest for a few minutes after cooking allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender and juicy final product.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a showstopper on its own, but it truly shines when served with traditional accompaniments.

*   **Breads:** Warm naan bread, roti, or paratha are perfect for scooping up the chicken and its delicious juices.
*   **Rice:** A side of fragrant basmati rice or a simple jeera rice (cumin rice) complements the robust flavors of the chicken.
*   **Chutneys & Dips:** A cooling mint-coriander chutney is an absolute must. A simple onion salad with lemon and green chilies, or a raita (yogurt dip) with cucumber, also provides a refreshing contrast.
*   **Freshness:** Always serve with plenty of fresh lemon wedges for squeezing over the hot chicken and a sprinkle of chopped fresh coriander.
*   **Beverages:** Wash it down with a glass of refreshing mango lassi or a chilled beer.

## Variations & FAQs

While Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a classic, there are ways to adapt it and common questions that arise.

**Variations:**
*   **Other Proteins:** The tandoori marinade is incredibly versatile. Try it with fish fillets (Tandoori Fish), paneer cubes (Tandoori Paneer), or even mixed vegetables for a vegetarian tandoori platter. Adjust cooking times accordingly.
*   **Oven/Gas Grill:** If a charcoal grill isn't an option, you can still make delicious tandoori chicken. Cook it in a hot oven (400-425°F or 200-220°C) on a wire rack over a baking sheet, or on a gas grill. While you might miss some of the deep smokiness, the flavor will still be fantastic.

**FAQs:**

*   **Can I make it without a charcoal grill?** Yes, as mentioned above, an oven or gas grill are good alternatives. For oven cooking, broil for the last few minutes to get a nice char.
*   **How long should I marinate the chicken?** A minimum of 4 hours, but 12-24 hours in the refrigerator yields the best results.
*   **Is Tandoori Chicken very spicy?** The spice level is adjustable. You can reduce or increase the red chili powder to suit your preference. Kashmiri chili powder offers a beautiful red color with less heat.
*   **Can I use boneless chicken?** Absolutely! Boneless chicken thighs or breasts work well. Just be sure to reduce the cooking time to prevent them from drying out. They will typically cook faster than bone-in pieces.
*   **What if I don't have mustard oil?** While mustard oil adds an authentic pungent flavor, you can substitute it with any neutral oil like vegetable or canola oil.

Embrace the sizzle and the smoke, and get ready to transform your dinner table with the irresistible flavors of Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled. This iconic Indian dish is more than just a recipe; it’s an invitation to explore the rich culinary heritage of India, bringing a taste of authentic tradition right into your home. Happy grilling!
